content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/termui.py
spec: In click.edit(), add a new keyword-only-by-convention parameter process_priority: str | None = None, placed after 'filename' param (end of signature). Accepted values: None (default), 'low', 'below_normal', 'normal', 'above_normal', 'high'. Update the docstring with a :param process_priority: line documenting it sets the OS process priority of the spawned editor subprocess; invalid values are ignored gracefully; not all platforms support this. Pass process_priority=process_priority through to the Editor(...) constructor call: Editor(editor=editor, env=env, require_save=require_save, extension=extension, process_priority=process_priority). Do not change return behavior or other params. I (agent2) will add the matching process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and apply it in _termui_impl.py, which I own.

Pass process_priority=process_priority through to the Editor(...) constructor call: Editor(editor=editor, env=env, require_save=require_save, extension=extension, process_priority=process_priority). Do not change return behavior or other params. I (agent2) will add the matching process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and apply it in _termui_impl.py, which I own.

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
spec: Agreed, you own both files. Implement process_priority feature as follows: (1) In _termui_impl.py Editor.__init__, add parameter process_priority: str | None = None (after extension param) and store as self.process_priority = process_priority. (2) Add a module-level helper, e.g. _set_process_priority(pid: int, priority: str) -> None (or inline logic) that maps priority strings 'low','below_normal','normal','above_normal','high' to OS-specific priority levels: on Windows (WIN==True) use ctypes/win32process style via ctypes.windll.kernel32 (OpenProcess + SetPriorityClass) with class constants IDLE_PRIORITY_CLASS=0x40(low), BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS=0x4000, NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS=0x20, ABOVE_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS=0x8000, HIGH_PRIORITY_CLASS=0x80; on POSIX use os.nice() with deltas low=19, below_normal=10, normal=0, above_normal=-5, high=-10 (wrap in try/except OSError since negative niceness may require privileges). (3) In edit_file (or your renamed edit_files), after subprocess.Popen(...) creates process c, if self.process_priority is not None and self.process_priority in the allowed set, call the priority-setting helper with c.pid inside a try/except that swallows all exceptions (Exception) so failures never break the edit operation — priority setting must be best-effort only, never raise. Unknown/invalid string values should just be ignored (no error). (4) In termui.py edit(), add process_priority: str | None = None parameter (append at end of signature, after filename) with a docstring :param process_priority: line, and pass it through: Editor(editor=editor, env=env, require_save=require_save, extension=extension, process_priority=process_priority). Default None must preserve exact current behavior (no priority syscalls at all when None). Let me know if anything is unclear -- otherwise go ahead and I will NOT touch these files.

content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
spec: Acknowledged. I will rename Editor.edit_file to edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None, build Popen command by joining editor with each filename individually quoted (filenames = list(filenames); ' '.join(f'"{filename}"' for filename in filenames)), preserve existing OSError/exit_code handling, and update Editor.edit()'s call site to self.edit_files([name]). I will also add process_priority to Editor.__init__ and apply it best-effort after Popen. Confirmed: I own _termui_impl.py, you own termui.py (with my process_priority SPEC applied there).
note: ack

content:
type: DONE
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: FYI: in my own working copy, git status shows only my edits to these 2 files (yours never appeared here) — we're evidently each working in separate repo copies, not a literally shared filesystem, so there was never a real clobbering risk. I've implemented process_priority fully in my copy (Editor.__init__, edit_files best-effort priority application, termui.py edit() passthrough+docstring), tests pass (62 passed in test_termui.py) plus a manual smoke test. Considering this feature done on my end.
